label: neither

label: offensive

label: hate

label: hate
rationale: Expresses approval for harm against a community: 'We don't care how many Kikuyus will be displaced or killed in post election violence'.

label: hate
rationale: Directly echoes approval for ethnic violence: 'We don't care how many Kikuyus will be displaced or killed'.

label: hate
rationale: Collectively degrades an ethnic group ('Stupid Luos') and mocks their living conditions.

label: neither

label: neither
rationale: Gloating about a community's loss of political power ('I really PITY KIKUYUS IN 2027'), which falls under robust political/electoral discourse without contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Ordinary political inquiry and tribal arithmetic regarding a community's voting behavior ('most troublesome when it comes to Luhya community unity').

rationale: Pure ethnic-bloc electoral analysis and voting arithmetic ('Bukusus have always voted 4 the sitting government').

rationale: Insults a subset of a community ('low budget entitled Kikuyus') based on their political speech rather than attacking the group as a whole.

label: hate
rationale: Collectively attributes negative characteristics to communities: 'luos na wakikuyu are the most unreliable, ubebewa akili sana' (brainwashed/manipulated).

label: hate
rationale: Declares an entire community inherently evil: 'THERE'S NOTHING BEYOND THE EVILS OF KALENJINS'.

rationale: Ordinary ethnic-bloc banter and political maneuvering ('trick Luos', 'Treasury ni yetu') without expressing collective hate.
